name: Promeropidae
description: Sugarbirds are a small family of passerine birds endemic to southern Africa. They are specialized nectar-feeders with long, decurved bills and elongated central tail feathers, ecologically similar to sunbirds and honeyeaters.

children.name: Promerops
children.description: A genus of long-tailed, nectar-feeding birds endemic to southern Africa.

children.name: Promerops cafer
children.description: The Cape Sugarbird is a large, brownish bird with a very long, graduated tail and a slender, curved bill. It is endemic to South Africa's fynbos region, feeding primarily on Protea nectar.

children.name: Promerops gurneyi
children.description: Gurney's Sugarbird resembles the Cape Sugarbird but has a shorter tail and more rufous underparts. It inhabits montane forests and fynbos in eastern Zimbabwe, Mozambique, and South Africa.
